title: 2026 Oscar predictions: Best Cinematography - No Film School
text: Sinners (DP: Autumn Durald Arkapaw) is positioned as the frontrunner. The cinematographer, known for Black Panther: Wakanda Forever, collaborated with Ryan Coogler on this 1930s Southern vampire gangster musical. Arkapaw's use of IMAX 65mm film to capture the expansive, gothic Delta landscape combined with moody interiors makes it the technical favorite. One Battle After Another (DPs: Michael Bauman & Paul Thomas Anderson) represents the auteur's vision, shot on Beaumont VistaVision camera and 65mm film—described as an automatic prestige contender. Hamnet features cinematographer Łukasz Żal, a two-time Oscar nominee for black-and-white work, teaming with director Chloé Zhao. F1: The Movie showcases Claudio Miranda's Oscar-winning expertise with pioneering custom camera systems mounted directly on Formula 1 vehicles. Avatar: Fire and Ash could surprise with its innovative motion capture and 3D camera work using the Sony VENICE with Rialto 3D system. The author's predicted final five ranks Sinners first, followed by One Battle After Another, Hamnet, F1: The Movie, and Avatar: Fire and Ash.
